heading: Remediation of medical service lines
label: 1706A Remediation of medical service lines
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). , inserted “of this title” after “section 1703(e)(1)” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–251, § 211(a)(6)(A)Subsec. (d)(1). , substituted “subsection (a)” for “paragraph (1)”.Pub. L. 115–251, § 211(a)(6)(B)
amendments: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). , inserted “of this title” after “section 1703(e)(1)” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–251, § 211(a)(6)(A)Subsec. (d)(1). , substituted “subsection (a)” for “paragraph (1)”.Pub. L. 115–251, § 211(a)(6)(B)

children.text: Increasing personnel or temporary personnel assistance, including mobile deployment teams.
Special hiring incentives, including the Education Debt Reduction Program under subchapter VII of chapter 76 of this title and recruitment, relocation, and retention incentives.
Providing improved training opportunities for staff.
Making structural modifications to the facility used by the medical service line.
Such other actions as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—Responsible Parties
children.label: b .—Responsible Parties
children.text: In each assessment submitted under subsection (a) with respect to a medical service line, the Secretary shall identify the individuals at the Central Office of the Veterans Health Administration, the facility used by the medical service line, and the central office of the relevant Veterans Integrated Service Network who are responsible for overseeing the progress of that medical service line in complying with the standards for quality established by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Interim Reports
children.label: c .—Interim Reports
children.text: Not later than 180 days after submitting an assessment under subsection (a) with respect to a medical service line, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the progress of that medical service line in complying with the standards for quality established by the Secretary and any other measures the Secretary will take to assist the medical service line in complying with such standards for quality.

submit to Congress an analysis of the remediation actions and costs of such actions taken with respect to each medical service line with respect to which the Secretary submitted an assessment and plan under subsection (a) in the preceding year, including an update on the progress of each such medical service line in complying with the standards for quality and timeliness established by the Secretary and any other actions the Secretary is undertaking to assist the medical service line in complying with standards for quality as established by the Secretary; and
publish such analysis on the internet website of the Department.
